is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study analyses P. falciparum isolates; inclusion required 'positive blood smear with P. falciparum mono-infection'.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr codons 51, 59, 108 and Pfdhps codons 437, 540 — all curated target positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 3 gives WT/MX/MT counts and percentages per codon (e.g. codon 108: MT 75 (63.6%); 437: MT 105 (89%); 540: MT 88 (74.6%)), out of 118 successful assays.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples collected at Kasangati Medical Complex, Wakiso district, 20 km NE of Kampala — a single named sub-national site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Clinical/parasitological work carried out in 2000; samples collected on day 0 during year 2000, bounding collection to a single year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Blood samples on filter paper from 169 malaria patients in Kasangati, Uganda — African clinical field isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary clinical study with molecular genotyping of field isolates and original efficacy follow-up.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Single-site field collection at Kasangati; mentions other Ugandan studies (Dorsey Mulago, nine sentinel sites) for comparison but these are distinct datasets, not overlapping samples.

supplement.evidence: All per-codon frequencies are in Table 3 within the main text; no supplementary tables referenced.

assessment.summary: Primary clinical study at Kasangati Medical Complex, Wakiso district, Uganda (samples collected in 2000). Reports extractable WT/MX/MT frequencies (n=118 successful assays) for Pfdhfr codons 51, 59, 108 and Pfdhps codons 437, 540 in Table 3, plus haplotype data in Table 4. Single sub-national site and single-year collection satisfy resolution criteria. All criteria met.
